title: chat_display and message_bodies cached in AppState: avoids per-frame work in egui's 60fps update loop
description: AppState maintains chat_display (precomputed display labels) and message_bodies (control-char-stripped bodies) as parallel Vecs updated only in apply_event, not recomputed in update(). In egui, update() runs every frame (typically 60fps). Computing strip_control_chars on every message every frame, or re-building display name + unread-count label strings for every chat every frame, would allocate on every frame and burn CPU for no reason. The caches are invalidated precisely when data changes: rebuild_chat_display() is called only from ChatsLoaded and ChatsDelta arms; message_bodies is rebuilt only from MessagesLoaded. A future contributor might inline these computations into the render function 'to keep AppState simple', not realising they are reintroducing per-frame allocation in a hot path.
